title: glehnia littoralis subsp. leiocarpa
intro: Glehnia littoralis subsp. leiocarpa, also known as Sea Cabbage or Glehnia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae), native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Glehnia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote digestion, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the roots of Glehnia are typically dried and used as a decoction or infusion, or they are combined with other herbs to create a tonic or tea.
